channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: U.S. launches fresh wave of strikes on Iran, as analysts warn conflict risks becoming 'forever war'
item.description: Fighting in the Middle East has intensified in recent days, but could escalate further if Iran fails to cooperate, President Donald Trump said.

item.title: Warren Buffett tells CNBC he initiated Berkshire Hathaway's investment in Alphabet
item.description: Warren Buffett said he was the driving force behind the conglomerate's recent big investment in Alphabet.

item.title: Morgan Stanley posts record quarterly revenue and profit as equities trading surges 69%
item.description: Morgan Stanley is expected to benefit from higher trading and investment banking revenue in the quarter, as rivals JPMorgan Chase and Goldman Sachs have shown.

item.title: Star analyst Dan Ives forms Yorkville Ives merchant bank after leaving Wedbush
item.description: The new firm, Yorkville Ives & Co., will combine investment banking, equity research, institutional trading and principal investing.

item.title: IBM just had its worst day ever. Oppenheimer thinks investors should stay away from the stock
item.description: The investment firm downgraded IBM to perform from outperform.

item.title: Stocks making the biggest moves premarket: BlackRock, PayPal, ASML, Morgan Stanley & more
item.description: These are the stocks posting the largest moves premarket.

item.title: PayPal jumps 20% in premarket on report of Stripe, Advent $53 billion takeover offer
item.description: Stripe and Advent International have reportedly made a joint offer to acquire PayPal Holdings Inc for $60.50 per share, in a deal that would value the payments company at more than $53 billion.

item.title: J&J quarterly earnings beat Wall Street estimates on strength of medicines unit
item.description: Johnson & ⁠Johnson also increased its full-year sales and ⁠profit ‌forecast.

item.title: Mortgage rates rise to highest level in nearly a year, causing homebuyers to pause
item.description: Mortgage rates moved higher last week, causing buyers to pull back, but refinancing did see small gains.

item.title: Warsh pledges Fed policy 'regime change' to rid inflation 'tax' on American people
item.description: Warsh pledged Tuesday to "get monetary policy right" and defeat the inflation that has bedeviled the central bank for the past five years.

item.title: Trump's attorney general nominee Todd Blanche faces Senate Judiciary questions
item.description: Todd Blanche previously served as a criminal defense lawyer for President Donald Trump.

item.title: Bipartisan group of senators propose Social Security reform process ahead of funding shortfall
item.description: Social Security's trust fund that helps pay retirement benefits may run out in about six years. A bipartisan group of senators is calling for earlier reform.

item.title: The AI boom just found two new winners: Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan Chase
item.description: Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan showed that Wall Street is a major beneficiary of the AI boom, with record revenue driven by surging trading and investment banking.

item.title: Warren Buffett calls Bill Gates' actions with Epstein 'distasteful,' but people make mistakes
item.description: The Berkshire Hathaway chairman left out the Gates Foundation from his annual charitable stock gifts for the first time in 20 years.

item.title: ASML rises 3% after hiking sales forecast for second time this year on strong AI chip demand
item.description: ASML on Wednesday raised its guidance for a second time this year as its customers continue to ramp up their production capacity of AI chips.

item.title: Current and former employees sue Meta, alleging discrimination in using AI to conduct layoffs
item.description: The lawsuit filed by current and former Meta employees underscores rising concerns about AI's impact on jobs and people with disabilities in the workforce.

item.title: Lucid dismisses report that it is weighing filing for bankruptcy or going private after shares plunge
item.description: The report said Lucid was considering options that could include going private or filing for bankruptcy protection.

item.title: Morgan Stanley likes these stocks heading into second-quarter earnings
item.description: GE Vernova, Lam Research and United Airlines are some of the stocks that could reward investors this earnings season, according to Morgan Stanley.

item.title: New York becomes first U.S. state to impose AI data center ban
item.description: New York became the first state to ban construction of 'hyperscale' AI data centers, for one year, after Gov. Kathy Hochul signed an executive order Tuesday.

item.title: Johnson & Johnson has a chance to show it's more than just a rotation winner
item.description: The healthcare giant's second-quarter earnings will shine the spotlight on its exciting new products and pipeline.

item.title: Apple in talks with startup that shrinks AI models to run on an iPhone
item.description: PrismML says its compressed version of Alibaba’s Qwen model uses up to 15 times less memory, potentially advancing Apple’s AI push.

item.title: Home prices hit a new record high — these 3 strategies can help you save
item.description: Choosing specific lenders and loan types, plus improving your credit, can help you save.

item.title: Renewed Hormuz hostilities drive ECB rates rethink amid ‘extremely volatile’ outlook
item.description: Several consecutive days of strikes exchanged between the U.S. and Iran have cast uncertainty on the European Central Bank's interest rate decision next week.

item.title: Jim Cramer says concerns about AI market froth are overblown. Here's why
item.description: CNBC's Jim Cramer said today's stock market is far less concerning than it was during the dot-com bubble.

item.title: Why Europe is suddenly betting big on drones
item.description: Drones and autonomous systems are moving from niche battlefield tools to a core part of modern warfare.

item.title: Here’s the inflation breakdown for June 2026 — in one chart
item.description: The consumer price index rose 3.5% in June from a year earlier, a deceleration after several months of upward moves.

item.title: Zipline adds ex-Tesla, Uber, Waymo execs to make drone delivery mainstream across U.S.
item.description: Zipline is growing its drone delivery business in the U.S., and has hired former Tesla, Uber Eats and Waymo executives to help it scale up in new markets.

item.title: U.S. trade official says 'very few' Nvidia H200 AI chips have been shipped to China
item.description: The remark is a sign that H200 shipments to China have restarted, potentially boosting Nvidia's sales even higher.

item.title: Paramount still plans to close WBD merger by end of September despite lawsuit
item.description: Paramount is still planning for its acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery to close by late September despite lawsuit challenge from state attorneys general.

item.title: China posts slowest quarterly growth since 2022 as investment slumps, fanning stimulus calls
item.description: That second-quarter growth came below Beijing's full-year growth target range of 4.5% to 5%, the least ambitious goal in decades.
